MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds (Cgc-13-276169)
Off Calendar Aug-23-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion to Apportion and Distribute Funds generated from plaintiffs' survival/wrongful death lawsuit and claims against asbestos bankrupt defendant trusts.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Jennifer Alesio, Esq. of Brayton Purcell appeared to present the issues.
James Rodriguez, as wrongful death heir, was present, placed under oath and testified.
Janice Rodriguez on behalf of disabled adult Robert Rodriguez, Jr., was present, placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by Wrongful Death Heirs Leslye Fazio, Monica Rodriguez, Michelle Walker, Lydia Moore, Andrew Moore, or Mason Moore.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from the survival/wrongful death lawsuit and claims against asbestos bankrupt defendant trusts negotiated after the July 14, 2012 death of Raymond Anchando in the following proportions as follows:

Estate of Raymond Anchando - 0%.
Estate of Raymond Moore - 0%.
Leslye Fazio - 50%.
James Rodriguez - 50%.
Robert Rodriguez, Jr. - 0%.
Monica Rodriguez - 0%.

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the July 14, 2012 death of Raymond Anchando shall be distributed in the percentages set forth on page 3, paragraph 1 of the Order approving the motion for apportionment.

Proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.
